title: Listera smallii
intro: Listera smallii, also known as Small's twayblade, is a perennial orchid native to North America, belonging to the genus Listera of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and combating infections. The bioactive constituents of Listera smallii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Listera smallii is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or infused oil, although its use is not widely documented and further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Listera smallii is a species of orchid in the genus Listera, commonly known as twayblades. It is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. Listera smallii is further grouped into the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Platantherae. This orchid species is native to eastern North America and is characterized by its small, delicate flowers and slender leaves. The classification of Listera smallii reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Orchidaceae family.

Additionally, Listera smallii has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis.
